testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_screenshot to verify page loaded without errors
Check page title or header contains 'PodcastBrain'
Verify a URL input field (text_input) is visible on the page

feature: URL input field accepts YouTube watch URLs
testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_fill the URL input field with 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jNQXAC9IVRw'
puppeteer_screenshot to confirm the URL text appears in the input field

feature: 'Download Audio' button is visible and clickable
testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_screenshot to verify 'Download Audio' button is visible
puppeteer_fill URL input with 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jNQXAC9IVRw'
puppeteer_click the 'Download Audio' button and verify no immediate Python traceback appears

feature: Progress bar increments from 0% to 100% during yt-dlp download
testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_fill the URL input with 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jNQXAC9IVRw' (short ~19s video)
puppeteer_click the 'Download Audio' button
puppeteer_screenshot immediately to confirm a progress bar element is now visible
Verify the progress bar shows a percentage or 'Downloading' status text
Wait for the download to complete (up to 60 seconds)
puppeteer_screenshot after completion to confirm the progress bar reached 100% or disappeared
Verify no Python traceback is visible in the page output

feature: Downloaded file appears in ./downloads/ directory with success message showing file path and size
testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_fill the URL input with 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jNQXAC9IVRw'
puppeteer_click 'Download Audio' and wait for completion
puppeteer_screenshot to verify a success message is displayed
Confirm the success message contains a file path string (e.g., './downloads/') and file size in MB
Run bash: ls ./downloads/ to confirm an .mp3 file exists with non-zero size

feature: Invalid URL shows a user-friendly error message (not a Python traceback)
testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_fill the URL input with 'not-a-real-url'
puppeteer_click 'Download Audio'
puppeteer_screenshot after a few seconds
Verify the page shows a red Streamlit error box with a readable message
Confirm no 'Traceback (most recent call last):' text is visible on the page

feature: Cancel button stops the active download and removes the partial file
testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_fill the URL input with a long YouTube video URL (>10 minutes to ensure download takes time)
puppeteer_click 'Download Audio' to start the download
puppeteer_screenshot to confirm progress bar is visible and download has started
puppeteer_click the 'Cancel' button while download is in progress
puppeteer_screenshot to confirm the download stopped and the UI reset to input state
Run bash: ls ./downloads/ to verify no partial .part file remains
Verify the app is responsive (URL input is editable again)

feature: Style: single-page layout has no overflow at standard 1280px window width
testing_steps: puppeteer_navigate to http://localhost:8501
puppeteer_evaluate to set window width to 1280px
puppeteer_screenshot and verify no horizontal scrollbar is visible and all UI elements fit within the viewport
